title: brief_meeting
description: Convert a meeting transcript into a four-section structured brief: my_asks (asks of the recipient), others_asks (asks the recipient is making of others), decisions (concrete commitments reached), and ambiguous_items (things left unresolved or unclear). Per plan.md §7 acceptance, ambiguous_items MUST quote the exact transcript span. v0.2.0 adds an optional `content_translation` field that turns each ask/decision/ambiguous item into an Input/Action/Goal-style scaffold so a neurodivergent reader gets the WHAT-TO-DO, not just the WHO-SAID-WHAT. The server orchestrates a structured prompt against the caller's MCP-client LLM; the server itself performs NO LLM calls.
$comment: v0.2.0 (2026-05-25): adds optional `content_translation` field. See describe_image.schema.json for the same field on that surface and `.claude-reports/2026-05-25-translate-not-summarize/REPORT.md` for the design.

transcript.description: Verbatim meeting transcript. Speaker labels SHOULD be present (e.g. 'Priya: ...') but are not required. Stored only in-memory for the duration of the call; never logged. The 200k-character cap handles roughly a 90-minute meeting; longer transcripts SHOULD be chunked by the caller.
examples:
Priya: Let's get the rollout date on the calendar. Thomas, can you own the migration script?
Thomas: Yes — I'll have it ready by Wednesday.
Roberto: We should also think about rollback. Can someone draft the runbook?
Priya: We can revisit that next week.

me.description: Speaker label or name that identifies the recipient ('me') in the transcript. Used to partition my_asks vs others_asks. Required — without it the four-section partition is ill-defined.

project.description: Optional project context. When supplied, the prompt template biases extraction toward asks and decisions relevant to this project and may be cross-referenced with mcp-cognitive-graph in downstream skills.

speakers.description: Optional roster of speaker labels present in the transcript. Helps the model disambiguate when speaker labels are inconsistent (e.g. 'Pri' vs 'Priya'). Capped at 30.

my_asks.description: Concrete asks the transcript places on 'me' (the recipient identified by input.me). Empty array when none. Ordered by transcript appearance.

others_asks.description: Concrete asks 'me' is making of others, or that one third party is making of another. Empty array when none. Ordered by transcript appearance.

decisions.description: Concrete decisions or commitments reached during the meeting. Each is verbatim-anchored to a transcript span. Ordered by transcript appearance.

ambiguous_items.description: Things the transcript leaves unresolved, deferred, or unclear. Each item MUST quote the exact transcript span verbatim (verbatim=true and quoted_span populated). Items where verbatim is false are not permitted in v0.1.0 — the field exists for forward-compatibility with summarised ambiguity that might land in v0.2. Ordered by transcript appearance.

content_translation.description: v0.2.0 — Optional reader-translated scaffold of the meeting. Each entry corresponds to a single ask, decision, or ambiguous item and decomposes it into Input/Action/Goal facets the reader can act on. WHEN populated, each entry's `label` SHOULD identify which list item it translates (e.g. 'my_asks[0]: own the migration script'). Set to null OR empty array when the brief is a chat-only meeting with nothing to act on. The legacy four-section fields (my_asks/others_asks/decisions/ambiguous_items) still fire either way for back-compat; content_translation is the field a neurodivergent reader reads when they want the WHAT-TO-DO scaffold, not the WHO-SAID-WHAT extraction.

eval_corpus_slice.description: Identifier of the eval corpus slice that validates this prompt template. Required so that any prompt change can be re-evaluated in CI per plan.md §7. The brief_meeting slice ties to the 30-meeting pilot referenced in §7 acceptance.

model_provenance.description: Where the LLM call ran (local or cloud, model id). Surfaced so the user / extension can render the 'cloud mode' banner accurately.

TRANSCRIPT_REQUIRED.description: The transcript argument was missing or shorter than 20 characters.

TRANSCRIPT_TOO_LONG.description: Transcript exceeded the 200000-character cap (roughly 90 minutes of speech). Caller SHOULD chunk and call repeatedly.

ME_REQUIRED.description: The me argument was missing. Without it the four-section partition is ill-defined; the server refuses rather than guessing.

VERBATIM_ANCHOR_FAILED.description: The LLM returned an ambiguous_item whose quoted_span text could not be located in the transcript. The server rejects the entire response rather than fabricating; caller MAY retry. This is the verbatim-anchor enforcement required by plan.md §7.

MODEL_UNAVAILABLE.description: The caller's MCP client could not reach its configured LLM.

examples.name: short rollout meeting with one decision, one ask of me, one ambiguous item

input.transcript:
Priya: Let's get the rollout date on the calendar. Thomas, can you own the migration script?
Thomas: Yes — I'll have it ready by Wednesday.
Roberto: We should also think about rollback. Can someone draft the runbook?
Priya: We can circle back on that next week.

my_asks.text: Own the migration script and have it ready by Wednesday.

quoted_span.text:
Thomas, can you own the migration script?
Thomas: Yes — I'll have it ready by Wednesday.
Roberto: We should also think about rollback. Can someone draft the runbook?

decisions.text: Thomas owns the migration script with a Wednesday deadline.

quoted_span.text:
Thomas, can you own the migration script?
Thomas: Yes — I'll have it ready by Wednesday.

ambiguous_items.text: Rollback runbook ownership and timing — deferred to 'next week' with no named owner.

quoted_span.text: Priya: We can circle back on that next week.

examples.name: strategy meeting with two ambiguous items, no decision

input.transcript:
Director: I want us to revisit the Q3 priorities. The current list doesn't feel right.
Thomas: Which priorities specifically?
Director: We'll work through that offline. For now I'd like everyone to think about it before next Friday.
Priya: I'll loop back with thoughts on the architecture doc next week.

my_asks.text: Think about Q3 priorities before next Friday.

quoted_span.text: Director: We'll work through that offline. For now I'd like everyone to think about it before next Friday.

ambiguous_items.text: Which Q3 priorities the Director wants revisited — deferred to 'offline' without scope.

quoted_span.text: Director: We'll work through that offline.

ambiguous_items.text: Priya's architecture-doc feedback — 'next week' with no committed day.

quoted_span.text: Priya: I'll loop back with thoughts on the architecture doc next week.

compatibility.policy: Additive-only within v0.x.x. v0.2.0 added optional `content_translation` plus the `$defs.TranslatedEntry` / `$defs.TranslatedFacet` types. The verbatim-anchor requirement on ambiguous_items is part of the v0.1.0 contract and cannot be relaxed without a major bump. New ambiguity reason codes are minor bumps. Renaming or retyping any field requires a new major version with a parallel $id.
compatibility.side_effects: None. Pure read. The server stores no input and emits no telemetry. Persisting the brief to mcp-cognitive-graph is the caller's responsibility.
compatibility.idempotency: Server-side idempotent. LLM-side determinism depends on the caller's model and sampling settings; the verbatim-anchor check rejects responses whose spans cannot be located, which provides a soft consistency guarantee.
compatibility.privacy: local-only-by-default. The server itself does not call any provider. Meeting transcripts are highly sensitive (third-party speakers, internal strategy); cloud-mode for brief_meeting SHOULD surface a stronger consent prompt than other tools — that is the extension's responsibility, not this server's.

label.description: Heading/identifier of this item — typically references which list slot it translates (e.g. 'my_asks[0]: migration script') so the reader can map the scaffold back to the verbatim-anchored source field.

facets.description: Reader-translated facets — Input/Action/Goal scaffold for this item.

kind.description: Facet kind — same enum as describe_image.TranslatedFacet for cross-tool consistency. Unknown values MUST be treated as `context` by forward-compatible callers.

text.description: The translated text of this facet, in NeuroDock voice. Per-neurotype addenda may further constrain word count and idiom usage.

asker.description: Speaker who made the ask, or null when not attributable from the transcript.

due.description: Free-text due indicator as expressed in the transcript ('Wednesday', 'EOD', 'next sprint'). Null when no timing was given. Not parsed to a structured date in v0.1.0 — that is reserved for a future minor version.

quoted_span.description: Verbatim transcript span supporting this ask. The text field MUST be a substring of the input transcript at the named offsets.

decided_by.description: Speakers credited with the decision. May be empty when attribution is unclear.

quoted_span.description: Verbatim transcript span supporting this decision.

text.description: Plain-language description of what is ambiguous or unresolved.

verbatim.description: MUST be true in v0.1.0 — every ambiguous item is anchored to a verbatim transcript span. The field exists as a const for forward-compatibility with v0.2 summarised-ambiguity items; v0.1.0 callers can assert this is always true.

quoted_span.description: Verbatim transcript span. The text field MUST be a substring of input.transcript at the named offsets. The server SHOULD reject (VERBATIM_ANCHOR_FAILED) any LLM response where this condition is violated, rather than fabricating ambiguity.

reason.description: Reason code for the ambiguity. Callers MUST treat unknown values as 'other' (forward-compatibility).

start_char.description: Zero-indexed inclusive character offset into input.transcript.

end_char.description: Zero-indexed exclusive character offset. end_char > start_char.

text.description: Verbatim transcript text between the offsets. MUST equal input.transcript.slice(start_char, end_char). Surfaced redundantly so callers can render quotes without re-slicing.
